 - Watch next week: overload risk at tier 1 due B/C-heavy mix, residual billing from B/C subscribers, and whether enterprise remains inert.
+
+## Week 34 / Day 231
+
+- Cash: $1,668,611. Individual subscribers fell to 231 after 357 cancellations; new subscribers were 0. Enterprise remains at zero seats and no fresh enterprise messages.
+- Week 33 cash flow: subscription payments $19,885, ops about -$24,500, compute about -$6,451, capacity about -$2,835, lead acquisition -$7.
+- Remaining book is almost entirely high-tier S2: C 179 at avg $301.51, B 24 at avg $249, A 28 at avg $61.14.
+- Open enterprise threads are all stale agent-waiting offers from days 140-210; no new customer replies.
+- Changes:
+  - Reduced C from tier 5 to tier 4 to cut compute while keeping S2 C quality very high.
+  - Dropped capacity from tier 1 to tier 0 now that usage is down to 386k/week and subscriber count is 231.
+  - Cut global ops to $200/day, no targeted ops.
+  - Kept prices $49/$149/$299, A1/B2, quotas A60000/B200000/C500000, development $0, ads $0.
+- Watch next week: tier 0 overload risk, whether C tier 4 triggers high-value churn, residual C billing, and any delayed enterprise movement.
